description: Propositional structure over passages — the formal cousin of argument-diagramming: where #Supports says 'this passage gives reason to believe that one', these tags assert logical form. Snippets play propositions; connectives ride tags on snippet↔snippet LINKs. Conjunction reuses the bundle convention (#Implies:a — jointly-sufficient antecedents, same grammar as #Supports:a). Disjunctive antecedents need NO hyperedge: (A ∨ B) → C is exactly {A #Implies C, B #Implies C} — case analysis is two edges, so the one connective that looks like it demands arity 3 decomposes for free. Negation between passages is #Negates (the engine primitive CONTRADICTS anchors a passage to a CONCEPT; passage↔passage contradiction is framework vocabulary). Nothing here EVALUATES: well-formedness lint (a #Negates edge inside an #Implies:a bundle, circular implication chains) is F1 territory; truth-functional checking would be an enrich-side adapter, never an engine feature.

edgeTags.name: Implies
edgeTags.description: if this passage's claim holds, that one follows; a subtype names conjoint antecedents (#Implies:a — jointly sufficient, none implies alone), bare = sufficient on its own. (A ∨ B) → C is two bare edges: case analysis

edgeTags.name: EquivalentTo
edgeTags.description: these passages assert logically equivalent claims — each implies the other (the biconditional)

edgeTags.name: Negates
edgeTags.description: these passages assert contradictory claims — at most one can hold. Passage-level ¬; distinct from the engine's CONTRADICTS (passage vs concept) and from #Opposes (argues against, weaker than logical contradiction)
